payload.raw_value_text: Thirteen proposals were received on July 24, 2023
payload.description: Proposals received for Emergency and Scheduled Bus Services RFP
Number of bus firms recommended by Selection Committee for contract award
Contract term including options

tool_args.label: Emergency and Scheduled Bus Services - Contract #426684

payload.description: Award of competitively solicited and negotiated miscellaneous service contracts to furnish emergency and scheduled bus services on an as-needed basis for railroad passengers and/or crews between stations throughout MNR and LIRR New York and Connecticut operating territories during scheduled and emergency track outages and service disruptions.
Request for Proposal (RFP) was publicly advertised in the New York State Contract Reporter and Journal News and posted on MNR's website
Thirteen proposals were received for the Emergency and Scheduled Bus Services RFP
Metro-North Committee review of procurement actions
MTA Board vote to award the procurement actions
Date of the procurement agenda document

payload.claim_text: Procurement actions in Schedule G (Miscellaneous Service Contracts) are not subject to competitive bidding requirements of PAL 1209 or 1265-a.
payload.description: Schedule G miscellaneous service contracts are exempt from competitive bidding requirements

payload.claim_text: Zero-dollar contracts will be awarded to qualified firms with no guarantee of spend amount.
payload.description: Contracts awarded with zero-dollar base; actual spending based on services rendered as needed

payload.claim_text: CDOT will reimburse MNR 100 percent for the costs of bus service on the New Canaan, Danbury, and Waterbury branches.
payload.description: Connecticut Department of Transportation fully reimburses MNR for bus service on specified CT branches

payload.claim_text: The Selection Committee evaluated proposals using criteria: (1) Demonstrated understanding of Workscope requirements; (2) confidence level of proposer, commitment of resources, qualifications of key personnel, reliability including subconsultant services; (3) project plan; (4) past experience on similar projects; (5) cost.
payload.description: Five established selection criteria used by the Selection Committee for RFP evaluation

payload.claim_text: The MTA Department of Diversity and Civil Rights has established a zero percent DBE goal on this contract due to the lack of subcontracting opportunities and DBE firms in the marketplace.
payload.description: No DBE participation goal set for this contract

payload.claim_text: Academy was found to be responsible notwithstanding Significant Adverse Information (SAI) and the award of the contract is subject to the approval of such responsibility finding by the MTA Chief Administrative Officer in consultation with the MTA General Counsel.
payload.description: Academy Express LLC responsibility finding subject to additional approvals

payload.claim_text: In connection with a previous contract awarded to Community Coach, Community Coach was found to be responsible notwithstanding SAI pursuant to the All-Agency Responsibility Guidelines, and such responsibility finding was approved by the MNR Acting President in consultation with the MNR General Counsel in January 2018. No new SAI has been found relating to Community Coach.
payload.description: Community Coach prior responsibility finding from January 2018 with no new SAI
RFP for Emergency and Scheduled Bus Services was publicly advertised on June 13, 2023
Thirteen proposals were received for the Emergency and Scheduled Bus Services RFP on July 24, 2023
Board vote requested for approval to award the Emergency and Scheduled Bus Services contracts
Metro-North Committee review of procurement actions on March 25, 2024
Metro-North Railroad leads the procurement on behalf of itself and LIRR
LIRR participates in the joint procurement for bus services
Connecticut Department of Transportation will reimburse MNR 100 percent for bus service costs on New Canaan, Danbury, and Waterbury branches
MNR portion of estimated aggregate budget for bus services
LIRR portion of estimated aggregate budget for bus services
Total estimated aggregate budget for bus services
MNR Zone 1 - New Haven to Bridgeport (Waterbury Branch)
MNR Zone 2 - Bridgeport to State line (Danbury and New Canaan Branches)

Emergency and scheduled bus services will be provided in MNR Zone 2 (CT)

payload.claim_text: The purchases/contracts will result in obligating funds in the amounts listed. Funds are available in the current operating/capital budgets for this purpose.
payload.description: Budget impact confirmation that funds are available in current operating/capital budgets

payload.claim_text: None. The Railroads do not have the resources to provide these services in-house.
payload.description: No alternatives to awarding bus service contracts as the railroads lack in-house resources

payload.claim_text: This procurement will be funded by each Railroad's respective Operating Budget, Capital Budget, the Connecticut Department of Transportation (CDOT) and/or FTA Funds, as the case may be.
payload.description: Multiple funding sources for the bus services procurement

payload.claim_text: Emergency busing is defined as the need for immediate service due to a sudden and unanticipated disruption. Scheduled busing is a request for service to supplement a planned disruption in scheduled train service because of capital and operating projects.
payload.description: Definitions of emergency vs scheduled bus services used in the procurement
Leprechaun Lines, Inc. serves MNR territory under the bus services contract

tool_args.label: Peter Pan Bus Lines serves MNR territory

payload.description: Peter Pan Bus Lines, Inc. serves MNR territory under the bus services contract
